System Design Copilot

Parallel interview prep flows for high-level design, low-level design, and DSA rounds, with guided lessons, case studies, and interactive practice labs.

Browse by flow

Switch interview mode

4 flows
High-level design interview prep HLD
Architecture, distributed systems trade-offs, and end-to-end system walkthroughs for classic system design rounds.
Low-level design interview prep LLD
Object modeling, extensible APIs, machine-coding structure, and follow-up handling for class-design rounds.
Data structures and algorithms interview prep DSA
Curated coding-round practice across general problem sets and company-specific question banks for array, tree, graph, DP, and mock-round prep.
AI Engineer learning path AI/ML
End-to-end AI/ML engineering from foundations through LLMs, RAG, agents, MLOps, and responsible AI for building production intelligent systems.

Curriculum → Data structures and algorithms interview prep

DSA interview flow

Data structures and algorithms interview prep

Use this flow when the interview expects coding, algorithm selection, complexity discussion, and live problem solving under follow-up questions.

Restate the problem, choose the pattern deliberately, defend the invariant or recurrence, then code the cleanest correct version before optimizing.

Flow progress 0/12

Lessons complete across 4 modules

Start here DSA foundations and warm-ups

Start with the patterns that dominate coding screens: arrays, strings, linked lists, trees, and the first layer of dynamic programming.

Best for Best for coding screens, online assessments, phone interviews, and onsite rounds centered on data structures and algorithms.

Alternate between one concept-focused module and one mock-style module so pattern study turns into timed execution.

Target outcome You should be able to identify common coding patterns quickly, explain trade-offs clearly, and complete structured mock rounds with less drift and fewer implementation mistakes.

Move through the modules in order so each later interview answer inherits a stable mental model.

4 modules 12 lessons DSA roadmap Open next module Browse modules

Core focus

What this flow trains

  1. Core DSA patterns such as arrays, hash maps, trees, graphs, and dynamic programming Keep tying the design back to the constraint or follow-up that makes this area matter in interviews.
  2. Execution habits for live coding: invariants, complexity, tests, and follow-up handling Keep tying the design back to the constraint or follow-up that makes this area matter in interviews.
  3. Company-specific practice sets built from Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Apple question banks Keep tying the design back to the constraint or follow-up that makes this area matter in interviews.
DSA specific Reusable module sequence

How to use it

Study rhythm

  • Open the next incomplete module and finish one lesson end to end.
  • Explain the main trade-off or object-model decision out loud before moving on.
  • Use the lesson practice lab to save a draft answer when the prompt calls for it.

Signals to build

What interviewers usually reward

  • Clear scoping and explicit assumptions before detailed design.
  • Clean boundaries between responsibilities, data, and side effects.
  • Trade-off awareness when requirements or follow-up constraints change.

Navigation

Keep the path obvious

  • Use the module runway below when you want the full sequence.
  • Use the global sidebar to jump between HLD and LLD at any time.
  • Track completion locally so interrupted practice sessions resume cleanly.

Module runway

Follow the DSA sequence from first module to capstone

Each module builds one interview muscle before the next one adds more complexity.

0/12 complete

Module

DSA foundations and warm-ups

Start with the patterns that dominate coding screens: arrays, strings, linked lists, trees, and the first layer of dynamic programming.

  • Recognize common coding-round patterns quickly from the prompt shape
  • Write a correct first-pass solution before optimizing under follow-up pressure
  • Explain invariants, edge cases, and complexity clearly while coding
3 lessons 0/3 complete

Module

Core patterns for coding rounds

Sharpen the medium-to-hard patterns that appear in stronger interview loops: sliding windows, recursion, graphs, and optimization-heavy DP.

  • Recognize the prompt signals that suggest windows, recursion trees, or memoization
  • Talk through state transitions cleanly before writing implementation details
  • Handle follow-ups that change constraints without restarting from zero
3 lessons 0/3 complete

Module

Company-specific DSA rounds

Practice question clusters that resemble Amazon, Google, and other large-company coding loops, using the company datasets directly.

  • Get comfortable switching between general patterns and company-specific question mixes
  • Rehearse explaining trade-offs in the style commonly expected in major tech interviews
  • Use company sets to simulate tighter, more targeted mock rounds
3 lessons 0/3 complete

Module

Mock loops and progression

Use warm-up, cross-company, and stretch sets to simulate a full DSA study progression from easy reps to harder follow-up-heavy rounds.

  • Build a repeatable practice cadence across easy, medium, and hard problem sets
  • Blend general and company datasets into realistic mixed mock rounds
  • Track which patterns still break down under time pressure
3 lessons 0/3 complete
